responses: Hello! I'm the CyberShield-AI assistant. Ask me anything about phishing or online safety.
Stay safe online! Feel free to come back if you have more questions.

Phishing is a cyberattack where attackers impersonate a trustworthy source (like a bank or well-known company) via email, text, or a fake website to trick you into revealing sensitive information such as passwords or card numbers.

patterns: how do i know if a site is phishing
how to identify a fake website
responses: Look for: misspelled domain names, no HTTPS padlock, urgent or threatening language, unexpected attachments/links, mismatched sender addresses, and requests for sensitive info. You can also paste a URL into the Dashboard here to get an automated check.

CyberShield-AI analyzes a URL using a trained machine learning model plus heuristics (URL structure, suspicious keywords), and can also check the site's SSL certificate, WHOIS domain age, and VirusTotal reputation to give you a combined risk verdict.

SSL/TLS certificates encrypt data between your browser and a website, and confirm the site's identity was verified by a certificate authority. A missing or invalid certificate is a red flag, though phishing sites can still have valid SSL certificates, so it's not proof of safety on its own.
WHOIS records show who registered a domain and when. Phishing domains are frequently registered only days or weeks before an attack, so a very new domain age is a common red flag.

patterns: what should i do after phishing
i entered my password on a fake site
responses: Act quickly: 1) Change the affected password immediately (and anywhere else you reused it), 2) Enable two-factor authentication, 3) Monitor your bank/card statements, 4) Report the site to your email provider or IT/security team, 5) Run a malware scan on your device if you downloaded anything.

patterns: where to report a phishing site
responses: You can report phishing sites to Google Safe Browsing, your email provider (e.g. 'Report phishing' in Gmail), or national bodies like CERT-In (India) or the APWG. Reporting helps get the site blocked for others.
I'm not sure about that one. Try asking about phishing signs, SSL/WHOIS checks, or what to do if you clicked a suspicious link.
